semantic.role: Infrastructure credential storage abstraction layer that defines a portable interface and provides platform-specific secret store backends for macOS Keychain, Linux libsecret, Windows DPAPI, and in-memory fallback.
responsibilities: Define the CredentialBackend interface for get/set/delete of secrets by service name and account.
Implement MacOSKeychainBackend using the security CLI tool and Swift keychain scripts.
Implement LinuxSecretToolBackend using the secret-tool command (libsecret).
Implement WindowsDPAPIBackend using PowerShell to call the Windows Data Protection API.
Implement MemoryCredentialBackend as a non-persistent in-process fallback.
Provide createDefaultCredentialBackend factory to select the appropriate backend based on os.platform().
outOfScope: Higher-level credential management (caching, encryption beyond OS DPAPI, credential rotation).
Network-based secret stores (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).
invariants: All backends must implement the CredentialBackend interface, ensuring a uniform contract for secret storage.
createDefaultCredentialBackend must return undefined when no suitable platform backend is available.
MemoryCredentialBackend secrets are not persisted across process restarts.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a clean, platform-agnostic credential storage adapter that isolates OS-specific secret management from the rest of the system.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Synchronized with accumulated source changes (release gate fixes); no documented architectural drift in this file.

publicSurface.description: Interface that defines get, set, delete operations for secret storage by service name and account. Used by credential store and any code that needs to persist secrets.
Concrete CredentialBackend implementation for macOS Keychain. Uses the security CLI and compiled Swift scripts to manage keychain items.
Concrete CredentialBackend implementation for Linux using the secret-tool command (libsecret D-Bus service).
Concrete CredentialBackend implementation for Windows using PowerShell to invoke the Data Protection API (CryptProtectData/CryptUnprotectData).
Concrete CredentialBackend implementation that stores secrets in a process-local Map. Not persisted across restarts; useful for testing or ephemeral environments.

Factory function that detects the current platform and returns an appropriate CredentialBackend instance, or undefined if none is available. Exported to be called by the credential store initialization.
